MASTER CALENDAR COURT

Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Set For Trial On Jan-16-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's demurrer to the sole cause of action for declaratory relief in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Richard Chu and Angela Zhao is overruled. Plaintiffs adequately allege facts showing that they face a risk of Wells Fargo violating the principal balance cap and their claim is not preempted by HOLA. The court declines to take judicial notice of exhibit P to Wells Fargo's request for judicial notice because the facts therein are reasonably subject to dispute and are in fact disputed by the first amended complaint. The court is required to accept the facts alleged in the first amended complaint as true on this demurrer. While it may be the minority view, the "growing trend," and in this court's view the better reasoned cases hold that "HOLA preemption only applies to conduct arising before a federal savings bank merged with a national bank association." (Beltz v. Wells Fargo Home Mortgage (ED CA 2017) 2017 WL 784910 *14 (quoting Harris v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (CD CA 2016) 2016 WL 341061 *8)). This view was adopted less than two months ago in a decision that acknowledges the split in authority and persuasively explains why HOLA preemption is inapplicable. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Continued To Jun-21-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Continued To Apr-27-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A's, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A'S Demurrer To Complaint is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for declaratory relief and sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for violation of the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. Plaintiffs Richard Yuk Lum Chu and Angela Xiao Zhao fail to adequately state a claim for declaratory relief because they have not alleged that Wells Fargo has demanded more than $1.25 million in principal or has threatened to demand such an amount. Plaintiffs' "fear" is an insufficient basis to support a declaratory relief claim. If they can do so in good faith, Mr. Chu and Ms. Zhao are granted leave to cure this deficiency. As to the second cause of action, Mr. Chu and Ms. Zhao fail to adequately state a claim under the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act because their claim is entirely dependent on Wells Fargo's alleged violation of 11 U.S.C. 506(b). However, by its terms section 506(b) applies only to "allowed claims." Because Mr. Chu and Ms. Zhao do not dispute that Wells Fargo never filed a proof of claim during Mr. Chu's bankruptcy proceeding, section 506(b) is inapplicable to Wells Fargo. (In re Blendheim (9th Cir. 2015) 803 F.3d 477, 485). Mr. Chu and Ms. Zhao are not granted leave to amend because there is no reasonable possibility that an amendment would cure this defect. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is transferred to Department 302 to be heard on February 9, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. All motions in this action to be noticed in Department 302 until further notice per the order of the presiding judge. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
